topic.what: Training-free vulnerability detection via multi-agent behavioral contract synthesis using Gherkin specifications
topic.how: Decomposes detection into three stages: Semantic Slicer extracts minimal vulnerability-relevant context (82.8% code reduction), Requirement Reverse Engineer synthesizes Gherkin behavioral contracts from code pairs, and Contract Judge performs strict compliance verification against specifications
topic.why_matters: Deep learning approaches catastrophically fail on rigorous benchmarks (F1 0.68→0.031) due to semantic ambiguity where identical code patterns can be secure or vulnerable depending on project-specific contracts. Phoenix restructures detection from open-ended classification to closed-form contract verification

characteristics.what_it_measures: Vulnerability detection F1 score on PrimeVul Paired Test Set
characteristics.vs_baseline: Prior SOTA RASM-Vul F1=0.668, VulTrial F1=0.563, Deep Learning models F1=0.031
characteristics.evidence: PrimeVul Paired Test Set with strict deduplication and temporal splitting
characteristics.context: Achieved with 7-14B open-source models vs 671B DeepSeek-V3 for RASM-Vul

characteristics.what_it_measures: Percentage of commit-level pairs where both vulnerable and patched samples correctly classified
characteristics.evidence: 280/435 pairs (8 pairs excluded due to XML failures)
characteristics.context: 3.2× improvement over multi-agent VulTrial using GPT-4o

characteristics.what_it_measures: Proportion of positive predictions that are correct
characteristics.context: Gherkin specification transforms task from pattern matching to contract verification

characteristics.what_it_measures: Proportion of actual vulnerabilities correctly identified
characteristics.context: Aggressive detection strategy with high recall

characteristics.what_it_measures: Parameter count relative to prior SOTA
characteristics.evidence: Phoenix uses fully open-source models up to 48× smaller
characteristics.context: Performance gap stems from problem restructuring, not model capacity

characteristics.what_it_measures: Percentage reduction in input code length via semantic slicing
characteristics.context: Agent 1 removes boilerplate to focus downstream agents

characteristics.what_it_measures: F1 improvement from adding Gherkin specifications vs sliced code only
characteristics.evidence: Consistent across all 5 model families evaluated
characteristics.context: Gherkin specifications identified as decisive performance driver

characteristics.what_it_measures: Proportion of false positives identifying genuine security concerns in patched code
characteristics.vs_baseline: Labeled as false positives by ground truth
characteristics.evidence: 17/97 FP cases represent real residual vulnerabilities (e.g., QEMU TODO comment confirming issue)
characteristics.context: Demonstrates that security is relative property defined against specific contracts, not absolute code property

characteristics.what_it_measures: Consistency of Gherkin impact across diverse model architectures
characteristics.vs_baseline: Consistent across general, code-specialized, and instruct models
characteristics.context: Gherkin provides structural scaffolding independent of model architecture
